sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Scanning is the foundation of everything in cortex — you can only query what has been indexed. All three original gaps have been closed: `cortex init` / `cortex build` are now distinct commands (init for first-run and reset, build for safe everyday use), AST call graph analysis has replaced manual dFlow `covers=` annotations for test→code linking, doc nodes now use `subtype='file'` and `subtype='section'` instead of the fragile `#`-in-ID convention, and hash-similarity rename detection automatically migrates history when a function moves between modules unchanged.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-SC-01 — First-time project setup**
As a developer, I want to run `cortex init` on a new project so that the index is created and all existing nodes have a baseline established in one step.

**US-SC-02 — Safe everyday build**
As a developer, I want to run `cortex build` after pulling or making changes so that newly added functions and modules are indexed without disturbing existing staleness signals.

**US-SC-03 — Reset without a separate command**
As a developer, I want `cortex init` to be safely re-runnable on an existing project so that I can reset the index to a clean baseline without needing to remember a separate command — `cortex init` prompts for confirmation if the DB already exists.

**US-SC-04 — Query project structure**
As an agent, I want to query all functions and modules in a project so that I can understand project structure without consuming context on raw file reads.

**US-SC-05 — Find callers and dependents**
As an agent, I want to find all nodes that depend on or call a specific function so that I can assess the blast radius of a change before making it.

**US-SC-06 — Keyword search**
As an agent, I want to search the index by keyword so that I can locate the relevant function or module without knowing the exact file path.

**US-SC-07 — Automatic rename detection**
As an agent, I want the index to automatically detect when a function was moved unchanged to a different module so that its history and verification record carry over to the new node ID without manual intervention.

**US-SC-08 — Correct STRUCTURAL_DRIFT on genuine changes**
As a developer, I want STRUCTURAL_DRIFT on genuinely deleted or refactored functions — not just moved ones — so that the index correctly distinguishes a move from a rewrite or split.

**US-SC-09 — Distinguish doc file nodes from doc section nodes**
As an agent, I want doc file nodes and doc section nodes to have distinct subtypes so that I can query one without the other and staleness logic does not rely on parsing the node ID string.

**US-SC-10 — Enumerate sections in a doc by parent ID**
As an agent, I want to call `cortex_list(parent_id=doc_id)` on a doc file node and get back its section nodes so that I can discover valid section IDs without falling back to reading the raw JSON file.

**US-SC-11 — Section IDs visible in rendered doc output**
As an agent, I want `cortex_render_doc` to annotate each section heading with its section ID so that I can read a doc and immediately know the IDs needed to call `cortex_update_section` — without a separate enumeration step.

sections.heading: V1 Requirements
sections.content:
**Project initialization & build:**
- [x] `cortex init` creates `.cortex/cortex.db`, runs a full scan, and establishes all `code_hash`/`desc_hash` baselines
- [x] `cortex build` is discovery-only (always safe): adds newly discovered nodes without disturbing existing staleness signals
- [x] `cortex init` is re-runnable: prompts for confirmation if DB already exists, then wipes and rebuilds all baselines
- [x] No `--full` flag on `cortex build` — the full-reset path is always `cortex init`

**Python AST scanning:**
- [x] Scans `.py` files via AST: extracts functions, async functions, methods, classes, and module-level metadata
- [x] Per-function `code_hash` (body without docstring) and `desc_hash` (docstring only) via SHA-256[:16]
- [x] Class methods get nodes prefixed with class name; nested functions get separate nodes with correct parent `composes` edge
- [x] Graceful skip on files with syntax errors (warning, not crash)

**DocJSON & Markdown scanning:**
- [x] DocJSON files scanned with section-level granularity: one `document` node per file, one per section
- [x] `documents` edges emitted from doc sections to linked code nodes via `links` arrays
- [x] Markdown files scanned with whole-file hashing
- [x] Doc file nodes use `subtype='file'`, section nodes use `subtype='section'` — replaces fragile `#`-in-ID convention
- [x] `composes` edges emitted from doc file node → each of its section nodes (enables `cortex_list(parent_id=doc_id)` to enumerate sections)
- [x] `cortex_render_doc` annotates each section heading with its section ID (enables agents to discover valid section IDs from a single read)

**Edge auto-generation:**
- [x] `composes` edges: module → each function/class defined in it
- [x] `validates` edges from AST call graph analysis: for test functions, resolve direct calls and attribute calls via enriched `name_map`, emit edges to indexed targets
- [x] Builder silently skips `validates` edges whose target is not indexed (no ontology violations)

**Decorator & metadata detection:**
- [x] dFlow decorators (`@workflow`, `@task`) detected via `_extract_dflow_meta` and stored as `dflow_meta`
- [x] External package imports indexed as `entity` nodes with `subtype='external_package'`

**Rename detection:**
- [x] On STRUCTURAL_DRIFT, check if any newly discovered node in the same build has the same `code_hash`
- [x] If match found: treat as move — migrate history and verification to new node ID, add `node_renames` row
- [x] If no match: leave as STRUCTURAL_DRIFT (genuine deletion or refactor)
- [x] Correctly distinguish: move (hash match → auto-rename), in-place refactor (same ID, new hash → DESC_STALE), split (no match → STRUCTURAL_DRIFT), deletion (no match → STRUCTURAL_DRIFT)

sections.heading: Non-Goals
sections.content:
The scanning sub-feature does NOT do any of the following in V1:

- **No JavaScript/TypeScript scanning** — Python only for per-function granularity. JS/TS is blocked on dFlow annotations extraction (ADR-006) and deferred to post-V1.
- **No import-graph edges** — `import` statements are not parsed into `imports` edges. Blast-radius queries use `delegates_to` and `validates` edges only.
- **No file watcher mode** — scanning requires an explicit `cortex build` invocation. Auto-rebuild on save via filesystem watcher (watchdog) is deferred.
- **No multi-language AST parsing** — non-Python files fall back to whole-file hashing.
- **No manual rename declaration** — V1 uses hash-similarity auto-detection only. `cortex link --rename old_id new_id` for edge cases (function renamed AND refactored) is icebox.
- **No automatic doc generation** — scanning detects and indexes; it does not generate docstrings or doc stubs.
- **No `cortex build --full`** — the full-reset path is `cortex init`. There is no destructive variant of `cortex build`.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Python AST scanner (functions, classes, modules) | Done |
| DocJSON section-level scanner | Done |
| Markdown doc scanner | Done |
| dFlow decorator detection (`@workflow`, `@task`, `@test_workflow`) | Done |
| `composes` edges (module→function) | Done |
| `validates` edges from dFlow `covers=` parameter | Done |
| Discovery-only mode (default — preserves staleness signals) | Done |
| External package entity nodes (`subtype='external_package'`) | Done |
| Graceful skip on syntax errors | Done |
| `cortex init` as distinct first-run command | Done |
| Hash-similarity rename detection | Done |
| AST call graph `validates` edges (no dFlow dependency) | Done |
| Doc node `subtype='file'` / `subtype='section'` distinction | Done |
| `composes` edges (doc file→section nodes) | Done |
| Section IDs annotated in `cortex_render_doc` output | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
~~**`cortex init` / `cortex build` split**~~ -- Completed. `cortex init` is the first-run command (creates DB, full baseline scan) and re-runnable for resets with confirmation. `cortex build` is always safe, discovery-only, no destructive variants.

~~**Hash-similarity rename detection**~~ -- Completed. When a function goes STRUCTURAL_DRIFT (not found in AST), the builder checks whether any newly discovered node in the same build has the same `code_hash`. If yes, it treats it as a move: migrates history and verification to the new node ID via `record_code_rename()`, adds a `node_renames` row. If no match, the node stays as STRUCTURAL_DRIFT (genuine deletion or refactor). Build output now reports `nodes_renamed` count.

~~**AST call graph test linking**~~ -- Completed. Static AST analysis at scan time replaces dFlow `covers=`. For each test function, the scanner builds an enriched `name_map` (tracking original function names for `from mod import func` bindings), walks the function body for direct calls (`func()`) and attribute calls (`mod.func()`), resolves each against the import map, and emits `validates` edges for indexed nodes. The builder silently skips edges whose target isn't indexed. No developer annotation required.

~~**Doc node subtype distinction**~~ -- Completed. File-level doc nodes now use `subtype='file'` and section-level doc nodes use `subtype='section'` in both `json_doc_scanner.py` and `doc_scanner.py`. The `subtype` column already existed in the schema. The fragile `if '#' in node.id` check in `viz/server.py` has been replaced with `if node.subtype == 'section'`. Requires `cortex init` to repopulate existing nodes with the new subtypes.

~~**`composes` edges for doc file → section nodes**~~ -- Completed. Both `json_doc_scanner.py` and `doc_scanner.py` now emit a `composes` edge from the file-level doc node to each of its section nodes, alongside the existing `documents` edge (section → file). This makes `cortex_list(parent_id=doc_id)` work for doc nodes exactly as it does for modules — agents can enumerate section IDs without falling back to raw file reads. Two new tests added to `tests/test_doc_subtype.py`.

~~**Section IDs in `cortex_render_doc` output**~~ -- Completed. `_render_doc_markdown` in `mcp_server.py` now appends `<!-- id: {section_id} -->` to each section heading in the rendered Markdown. Agents reading a doc via `cortex_render_doc` immediately see the section IDs needed to call `cortex_update_section` — no separate enumeration step required.

**JavaScript/TypeScript AST scanning** — Extend the scanner to parse `.ts` and `.js` files for frontend code. Per-function granularity using the TypeScript compiler API or a pure-JS AST parser (acorn/swc). Lower priority since the core use case is Python.

**Import-graph edge detection** — Parse `import` statements to generate `imports` edges between modules, enabling blast-radius queries that follow import chains rather than just explicit `delegates_to` edges.

**File watcher mode** — Auto-run `cortex build` when source files change, using a filesystem watcher (watchdog). Useful for keeping the index hot during active development sessions without manual re-runs.
